Usage

Some NPCs can fill multiple roles and will appear on the table in several locations. 

Melee characters are listed with a range of three levels, for example [1-3]. The NPC is of the median level; however by applying either the Elite or Weak template, you can use those NPCs for the higher or lower level listed in the range. 

Spellcasting characters are listed with a range of two levels, for example [1-2]. You'll find the actual NPC is the higher of the two levels. Given the increase in power and versatility that comes with learning a new level of spells, it is recommended that you do not use these NPCs with the Elite template, but they are readily compatible with the Weak template. Furthermore, given the adjustments applied by the Weak template to spell damage, I do not recommend adjusting the number of spell slots.

NPCs have been provided with a minimal amount of gear based on table 2-4: Safe Items from the Gamemastery Guide, focusing entirely on fundamental rune progression. While this means you can slot them into your game with a minimal impact on your party's treasure, if you are using them as the base for a significant foe you will want to expand upon or upgrade upon the gear provided, particularly in terms of consumables, worn or held magic items, and things like scrolls, staves and wands for spellcasting NPCs. The NPC statistics already reflect appropriate values for creatures of their level based on Paizo's creature creation guidelines, so if you are upgrading the fundamental rules (+x potency rules, resilience, striking) I would recommend exercising caution with changing those values, although applying the effects of property runes should be acceptable, especially if your players are level 5+. 

These NPCs are designed to have lower complexity than the NPCs with Classes and are particularly appropriate for use as supporting characters. For major named antagonists it may be beneficial to select one to two feats from an appropriate class, ancestry or skill to expand upon their versatility and make for a more complex villain. 

For higher level threats, the intention is that individual NPCs of this sort stop being a threat to the party, and you need to transition to using @UUID[Compendium.pf2e-mercenary-marketplace-vol1.mmv1-journals.1s34nyLF4vYUxsHX.JournalEntryPage.J5arLCsj7pbrI1eQ]{Crews}. I've intentionally not created any NPCs with jobs over level 12 to explicitly encourage this transition.

Design Philosophy

In making these NPCs I have split them into seven different combat roles. Each NPC was designed to fulfill one or more of these combat roles, while remaining true to the core identity of whatever job that NPC has. Thus, abilities have been designed such that the Tactician has abilities that feel appropriate for someone who makes their living as a tactician, while the Necromancer has abilities that feel appropriate for someone who raises the dead, even through both act as leaders. In comparison to the NPCs with Class, NPCs with Jobs have been generally designed to have fewer and simpler abilities to make them easier to run; and while these NPCs can make for great named antagonists they tend to be simpler in design making them especially suitable for use as lackeys.

Many of these NPCs have brand new abilities that are particularly suited to their theme, these abilities will tend to be more powerful than a player's ability of the same level to compensate for the relative lack of breadth these NPCs have compared to player characters. However, with some tweaking, an ambitious GM could turn these unique mechanics and the progression demonstrated between the lower and high level NPCs with jobs into player facing archetypes. In general, many of these abilities are intended to be player visible. What I mean by that is, they should be discoverable with recall knowledge, and that the abilities have been designed to be things the NPCs actively do, rather than passive bonuses. Where passive bonuses do exist I've endeavored to make them significant enough and obvious enough based on the concept that I hope your players will be able to guess to avoid a particular tactic or save based on what sort of NPC they are facing. 

I've only included NPCs with levels between 1 to 12 in this collection, with the notion that based on what Paizo has created, level 1 to 12 is a believable level of power for these more generic NPCs, and they could be feasible slotted into your adventure paths and Golarion games without breaking versamillatude. As your party moves into the mid to high levels individual NPCs give way to crews of NPCs to appropriately challenge your players, while creating a sense of realistic scaling in the scope and stakes of what your PCs are able to accomplish. With the Crew Templates these foes can thus remains viable threats up until the final few levels of your campaigns.

用法

鉴于FoundryVTT目前的限制，诸如虚弱与精英之类的模板不会影响行内伤害掷骰。由于行内掷骰是部队使用的主要伤害掷骰形式，因此每个部队只以单一等级呈现，而不是一个等级范围。除非你准备进行若干手动调整，否则我不建议尝试用虚弱与精英调整来修改你的部队。

部队以超巨型生物呈现以反映规则；然而，如果你希望部队能够按照“列队（Form Up）”中的规则改变其形状，你会希望将体型调整为中型，并放置16个中型指示物，全部链接到最初导入的角色。此设置允许你自由移动各个成员，同时仍然提供一个共享的生命条。如果你不想采用这种方法，而是更倾向于将部队视为一个超巨型生物，那么你很可能会希望将部队的等级视为低1级，尤其是在主要造成近战伤害的部队上。无法改变部队形状会对部队的伤害输出产生强烈的不利影响，因为它将无法调整位置来用其攻击覆盖队伍中的大多数人。对于主要依赖远程伤害或施法的部队，这种调整则没有必要。

部队创建指南

由于Paizo没有在《怪物图鉴3》中发布部队创建指南，我已尽我所能分析他们已发布的部队，并制定了以下指南。一般而言，你会希望遵循《主持人指南》第2章中的怪物创建指南。

  1. 部队的体型应为超巨型。
  2. 部队攻击的DC通常应使用《法术DC》表中的中等DC，并在约15级时提高到《法术DC》表中的高DC。如果你希望某个攻击特别难以抵抗，将难度提高一个档次，从中等提高到高（或从高提高到极限）；如果你希望它易于抵抗，则将难度降低一个档次。
  3. 对于以区域为目标、以所有相邻生物为目标，或以多个生物为目标的部队攻击动作，请遵循《主持人指南》表2-12中的“无限使用 区域效果 伤害”指南。单动作版本的攻击应造成推荐值的1/2，双动作版本应造成推荐值，三动作版本应造成约1 1/4到1 1/3倍的推荐值。
  4. 部队应具有等同于其等级生物最低值的溅射伤害弱点，其数值基于《主持人指南》表2-8中列出的指南。其区域效果伤害弱点则为上述数值的两倍（我个人建议用“部队 区域效果 易伤性（Troop Area Effect Vulnerability）”替代区域效果弱点）。鉴于这些弱点，你会希望在表2-7中更偏向选择高HP。此外，除非你的部队应该极其敏捷，否则你会希望其反射豁免不高于其等级生物的中等值。
  5. 该生物在12格与8格时的生命值阈值分别为其最大生命值的2/3与1/3。生成便于记忆的这些阈值数字的一种简单方法，是让该生物的最大生命值为15的倍数。

设计理念

我发现Paizo的部队对区域效果法术的弱点非常令人不满意：无论你施放的是1环的燃烧之手法术还是10环的流星雨，弱点产生的效果都相同。而我认为，用更高环的区域效果法术对部队施放，应该产生按比例更大的效果。为了解决这一困扰，我的方案是用“区域效果易伤性（Area Effect Vulnerability）”替换该弱点：它会使部队在对抗区域效果法术时降低其成功程度，从而使法术的效力能够随法术强度自然扩展。如果你更偏好标准的部队弱点，移除“部队 区域效果 易伤性（Troop Area Effect Vulnerability）”，并给予该部队一个数值等于其所列溅射伤害弱点两倍的弱点（针对区域效果伤害）。

在可能的情况下，我选择为这些部队创造能体现其名称所唤起之奇幻意象的能力。不过，和《雇佣兵市场》第一卷中收录的所有其他角色一样，我没有提供明确的背景条目，因为它们旨在被改编到你的世界中。

Troops

Here you'll find a catalogue of all the new troops included in Mercenary Marketplace Volume 1. In volume 1, troops have been produced for levels between 4 and 18.

Usage

Given the limitations of FoundryVTT at present, templates like Weak and Elite do not affect inline damage rolls. Given that inline rolls are the primary sort of damage roll used for troops, each troop is presented at a single level only rather than a level range. I don't recommend trying to modify your troops with the Weak and Elite adjustments unless you're prepared to make a number of manual adjustments.

Troops have been presented as Gargantuan creatures to reflect the rules, however, if you wish to have troops that can alter their shape per the rules in Form Up, you will wish to adjust the size to medium and place 16 medium tokens all linked to the initial imported actor. This set up allows you to freely move the individual members around while still providing a single shared health bar. If you not wish to take this approach and would instead prefer to treat the troop as a gargantuan creature, then you will likely want to treat the troops level as one level lower, especially in the case of troops that predominately deal melee damage. The inability to alter the shape of the troop will have a strongly detrimental impact of the troop's damage output, as it will be unable to shift around to catch most of the party with its attacks. For troops that mostly rely on ranged damage or spellcasting, this adjustment is unnecessary. 

Troop Creation Guidelines

Since Paizo didn't publish troop creation guidelines in Bestiary 3, I've done my best to analyze the troops they've published and produce the following guidelines. Generally, you will wish follow the monster creation guidelines from Chapter 2 of the Gamemastery Guide. 

  1. Troops should be gargantuan in size.
  2. The DCs for the troop attacks should generally use the Moderate DC from the Spell DC table, increasing to the High DC from the Spell DC table around level 15. If you want an attack to be particularly hard to resist, increase the difficulty by one step, from Moderate to High (or high to extreme) and if you want it to be easy to resist, decrease it by one step.
  3. For troop attack actions that target an area, all adjacent creatures, or target multiple creatures, follow the guidelines for unlimited use Area Effect Damage in Table 2-12 from the Gamemaster Guide. The single action version of an attack should deal 1/2 the recommended value, the two action version should deal the recommended value and the three action version should deal approximately 1 1/4 to 1 1/3 times the recommended value.
  4. The troop should have a weakness to splash damage equal to the minimum value for a creature of its level, based on the guidelines listed in Table 2-8 of the Gamemaster Guide. Double that for its weakness to area effect damage (I personally recommend substituting the area effect weakness for Troop Area Effect Vulnerability). Given these weaknesses, you'll want to err on the side of high HP from Table 2-7. Furthermore, unless your troop should be incredibly dexterous, you will want its Reflex saves to be no higher than the moderate value for a creature of its level.
  5. The creature's hit point thresholds for 12 squares and 8 squares are 2/3rds and 1/3rd of its maximum hit points respectively. An easy way to produce nice memorable numbers for these thresholds is to make the maximum hit points of the creature a multiple of 15.

Design Philosophy

I find the weakness of Paizo's troops to Area Effect spells to be very unsatisfying, the weakness has the same effect whether you spend a first level burning hands spell or a 10th level meteor swarm. Whereas I feel that a higher level area effect spell used on a troop should produce a proportionately greater effect. My solution to address this grip was is to replace the weakness with a Area Effect Vulnerability, which decreases the degree of success for troops against area effect spells, thus making their effectiveness naturally scale with the power of the spell. If you find you'd prefer the standard troop weaknesses, remove the Troop Area Effect Vulnerability and give the troop a weakness with a value equal to double its listed weakness to splash damage.

Wherever possible I've chosen to create abilities for these troops that reflect the fantasy evoked by the troop's name. Though, like all other actors included in Mercenary Marketplace Volume 1, I have not provided an explicit lore entry, as they are intended to be adapted to your world.

Optional Art Mapping.name: 可选艺术映射 Optional Art Mapping
Optional Art Mapping.text:
可选美术映射

如果你已安装 Pathfinder Tokens: Character Gallery，Mercenary Marketplace 现在包含一个可选的美术映射，可为除通用施法者和犬类动物伙伴之外的所有指示物提供美术。你可以通过前往 设置 -> 核心 -> 配置美术，并取消勾选 Mercenary Marketplace Volume 1 的 Token 和 Portrait 来停用此映射。

Optional Art Mapping

If you have the Pathfinder Tokens: Character Gallery installed, Mercenary Marketplace now contains an optional art mapping to provide art for all the tokens, except the generic spellcasters and the canine animal companion. You can deactivate this mapping by going to Settings -> Core -> Configure Art and unchecking Token and Portrait for Mercenary Marketplace Volume 1.
